task_completion.description: OpenOffice Writer is opened and the excavation report is started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Apache OpenOffice Writer is open with a document containing text related to the Coyote Springs Pueblo (LA 189274) excavation report.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles are used for document structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The formatting toolbar's style dropdown shows 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2' (not 'Default') when the cursor is on section titles, indicating proper style usage rather than manual bolding.

task_completion.description: An auto-generated Table of Contents is included in the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Table of Contents block is visible, typically displaying a gray background behind the text to indicate it is an auto-generated field, listing the document's headings.

task_completion.description: Page numbers are inserted into the document's footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numbers are visible in the bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages.

task_completion.description: Three specific data tables (artifact inventory, dating results, feature list) are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three distinct tables are visible in the document containing structured data for artifacts (e.g., ceramics), dating (e.g., AD 1198), and features.

task_completion.description: The document is saved as UNM-OCA-2024-031.odt in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenOffice Writer title bar displays 'UNM-OCA-2024-031.odt' or a Save dialog shows the file being saved to /home/ga/Documents/ with this exact name.

integrity.description: The agent used the Apache OpenOffice Writer GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through OpenOffice menus (e.g., Insert -> Indexes and Tables, Insert -> Footer) and typing in the application.

integrity.description: The agent extracted actual data from the JSON file rather than hallucinating.
integrity.visual_evidence: The document text and tables contain specific values like 'LA 189274', 'AD 1198', and 'AD 1050-1250' rather than generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by using terminal scripts to generate the .odt file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run Python scripts or pandoc commands that directly output the 'UNM-OCA-2024-031.odt' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be named 'UNM-OCA-2024-031.odt'. The document must contain specific data from the input JSON, including the site name 'Coyote Springs' (LA 189274), dates 'AD 1198' and 'AD 1050-1250', and keywords like 'pueblo', 'ceramic', and 'stratigraphy'. The final document should be substantial (ideally ~35 paragraphs, 8+ Heading 1s, 10+ Heading 2s) and contain exactly 3 tables (artifact inventory, dating results, feature list).
